-################################################################################

# Libraries
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(vegan)
library(picante)
library(ggordiplots)
#library(gridExtra)
library(cowplot)
library(viridis)
library(DESeq2)
#library(patchwork)
#library(EnhancedVolcano)
```

# Import data

## Horizontal coverage ('detection')
```{r}
data.fct.det <- readRDS("../rds/data.fct.det.rds")
rownames(data.fct.det) <- data.fct.det$gene_callers_id
```

## Coverage depth
```{r}
data.fct.cov <- readRDS("../rds/data.fct.cov.rds")
rownames(data.fct.cov) <- data.fct.cov$gene_callers_id
```

## Metadata
```{r}
metadata <- read.delim("../data/samples_metadata.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", dec = ".", row.names = 1)
metadata <- metadata %>% arrange(row.names(.))
metadata <- metadata %>% mutate(across(c(plant_clade,plant_order,plant_family,host_species,tree), as.factor))
metadata$host_species <- factor(x = metadata$host_species, levels = c("ABBA","THOC","ACSA","FAGR","COCO"))
metadata$shrub_tree <- c("tree","tree","tree","tree","tree","tree","tree","tree","tree","tree","shrub","shrub","shrub","shrub","shrub","tree","tree","tree","tree","tree","tree","tree","tree","tree","tree")
metadata$shrub_tree <- factor(x = metadata$shrub_tree, levels = c("shrub","tree"))
metadata$gymno_angio_tree <- c("gymnosperm_tree","gymnosperm_tree","gymnosperm_tree","gymnosperm_tree","gymnosperm_tree","angiosperm_tree","angiosperm_tree","angiosperm_tree","angiosperm_tree","angiosperm_tree","angiosperm_shrub","angiosperm_shrub","angiosperm_shrub","angiosperm_shrub","angiosperm_shrub","angiosperm_tree","angiosperm_tree","angiosperm_tree","angiosperm_tree","angiosperm_tree","gymnosperm_tree","gymnosperm_tree","gymnosperm_tree","gymnosperm_tree","gymnosperm_tree")
metadata$gymno_angio_tree <- factor(x = metadata$gymno_angio_tree, levels = c("gymnosperm_tree","angiosperm_tree","angiosperm_shrub"))
```

################################################################################

# Prepare data

## Detection

For analyses, keep only genes that are horizontally covered at least 1X for 50% of nucleotides. Those genes are considered 'present'.

If det<0.5 = 0, if det>=0.5 = 1
```{r}
data.det.PA <- data.frame(ifelse(data.fct.det[,c(42:66)] < 0.5, 0, 1))
```

## Coverage

For analyses, keep coverage values only for genes that are 'present', then round the coverage values to integers (counts of genes).

### Filter

If det=0, cov=0; if det=1, cov=cov
```{r}
data.cov.pre <- data.fct.cov[,c(42:66)]
data.cov <- data.frame(mapply(function(x, y) ifelse(x == 1, y, 0), data.det.PA, data.cov.pre))
rownames(data.cov) <- rownames(data.fct.cov)
```

### Round
```{r}
data.cov.c <- round(data.cov, digits=0)
```

## Statistics NON-RAREFIED DATA
```{r}
# detection stats
colSums(data.det.PA) # Richness of 'present' genes per sample (genes >= 50% horizontal coverage)
temp <- data.frame(ifelse(data.fct.det[,c(42:66)] < 0.1, 0, 1))
colSums(temp)
colSums(data.det.PA)/colSums(temp)
mean(colSums(data.det.PA)/colSums(temp)) # By removing genes that are < 50% horizontally covered (compared to removing genes less than 10% covered), we lose a mean of 63.1% of total genes richness per sample... WHATEVER THAT MEANS...
rm(temp)

# coverage stats
colSums(data.cov)/colSums(data.cov.pre) 
mean(colSums(data.cov)/colSums(data.cov.pre)) # By removing genes that are < 50% horizontally covered, we lose a mean of 25.1% of total coverage values per sample
colSums(data.cov.c)/colSums(data.cov) # Rounding coverage values to integers results in an extremely similar sum of coverages per samples
```

### Merge coverage data
Merge coverage counts dataframe with functions
```{r}
data.fct.cov.c <- cbind(data.fct.cov[,c(1:41)], data.cov.c)
```

### Aggregate by GC
Aggregate (sum) count values by GC
```{r}
data.fct.cov.c %>% distinct(gene_cluster_id) # There is 70,695 gene clusters in Methylobacterium pangenome
data.cov.c.agg <- aggregate(cbind(data.fct.cov.c$ABBA_M11,data.fct.cov.c$ABBA_M12,data.fct.cov.c$ABBA_M13,data.fct.cov.c$ABBA_M14,data.fct.cov.c$ABBA_M15,data.fct.cov.c$ACSA_M21,data.fct.cov.c$ACSA_M22,data.fct.cov.c$ACSA_M23,data.fct.cov.c$ACSA_M24,data.fct.cov.c$ACSA_M25,data.fct.cov.c$COCO_M01,data.fct.cov.c$COCO_M02,data.fct.cov.c$COCO_M03,data.fct.cov.c$COCO_M04,data.fct.cov.c$COCO_M05,data.fct.cov.c$FAGR_M16,data.fct.cov.c$FAGR_M17,data.fct.cov.c$FAGR_M18,data.fct.cov.c$FAGR_M19,data.fct.cov.c$FAGR_M20,data.fct.cov.c$THOC_M06,data.fct.cov.c$THOC_M07,data.fct.cov.c$THOC_M08,data.fct.cov.c$THOC_M09,data.fct.cov.c$THOC_M10),  by = list(data.fct.cov.c$gene_cluster_id), sum)
colnames(data.cov.c.agg) <- c("gene_cluster_id","ABBA_M11","ABBA_M12","ABBA_M13","ABBA_M14","ABBA_M15","ACSA_M21","ACSA_M22","ACSA_M23","ACSA_M24","ACSA_M25","COCO_M01","COCO_M02","COCO_M03","COCO_M04","COCO_M05","FAGR_M16","FAGR_M17","FAGR_M18","FAGR_M19","FAGR_M20","THOC_M06","THOC_M07","THOC_M08","THOC_M09","THOC_M10")
```

## Gene clusters statistics
```{r}
colSums(data.cov.c.agg[,c(2:26)])
plot(colSums(data.cov.c.agg[,c(2:26)]) ~ metadata$host_species)
```
We see on the plot that differences in total GC coverage among host is correlated with Methylobacterium species richness (see other boxplot in memoire)
We will rarefy (that was already the plan, but this support rarefaction).


## GC community matrix
```{r}
rownames(data.cov.c.agg) <- data.cov.c.agg$gene_cluster_id
data.cov.c.agg <- data.cov.c.agg[,-1]
data.cov.c.agg.comm <- as.data.frame(t(data.cov.c.agg))
data.cov.c.agg.comm <- data.cov.c.agg.comm [,which(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)!=0)] # 22,190 gene clusters with non zero coverage values
sum(data.cov.c.agg.comm) # 7,476,568 total GC counts
rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)
min(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm))
max(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm))
```


################################################################################

# TESTS : Rounding and aggregating tests


   ##########################################
   NO NEED TO RERUN CHUNKS BELOW (TESTS ONLY)

## Rationale for rounding, aggregating and level of rarefying approaches

In my data preparation, I make the assumption that the rounded coverage of GC in a given sample is a measure of its abundance (i.e. the number of 'hits' a gene or GC have)

Import reads tracking data
```{r}
reads.data <- read.delim("/mnt/storage/jocelyn/msc/msc_detection_coverage/data_imported/nb_reads.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", dec = ".", row.names = 1)
```

Two scenarios are possible to round coverage (at gene calls level, or GC level).

### Scenario 1 - gene calls level
Check if aggregated (summed) rounded counts of GC correlates with number of mapped reads
(first round, then aggregate) (this is what I did in the above chunks)

Linear model and plot
```{r}
summary(lm(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm) ~ reads.data$mapped_reads))
plot(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm) ~ reads.data$mapped_reads)
abline(-5.028e+04,1.415e-01)
```
The correlation between total number of GC count (hits) and the total number of mapped reads per sample is almost perfect (R2=99.89%) 

### Scenario 2 - GC level
Check if rounded aggregated (summed) counts correlates with number of mapped reads
(first aggregate, than round) (this is NOT what I did in my data preparation chunks above, it is only to test if the rounding approach makes a difference)

Data preparation
```{r}
temp1 <- cbind(data.fct.cov[,c(1:41)], data.cov)
data.cov.agg.c <- aggregate(cbind(temp1$ABBA_M11,temp1$ABBA_M12,temp1$ABBA_M13,temp1$ABBA_M14,temp1$ABBA_M15,temp1$ACSA_M21,temp1$ACSA_M22,temp1$ACSA_M23,temp1$ACSA_M24,temp1$ACSA_M25,temp1$COCO_M01,temp1$COCO_M02,temp1$COCO_M03,temp1$COCO_M04,temp1$COCO_M05,temp1$FAGR_M16,temp1$FAGR_M17,temp1$FAGR_M18,temp1$FAGR_M19,temp1$FAGR_M20,temp1$THOC_M06,temp1$THOC_M07,temp1$THOC_M08,temp1$THOC_M09,temp1$THOC_M10),  by = list(temp1$gene_cluster_id), sum)
colnames(data.cov.agg.c) <- c("gene_cluster_id","ABBA_M11","ABBA_M12","ABBA_M13","ABBA_M14","ABBA_M15","ACSA_M21","ACSA_M22","ACSA_M23","ACSA_M24","ACSA_M25","COCO_M01","COCO_M02","COCO_M03","COCO_M04","COCO_M05","FAGR_M16","FAGR_M17","FAGR_M18","FAGR_M19","FAGR_M20","THOC_M06","THOC_M07","THOC_M08","THOC_M09","THOC_M10")
rownames(data.cov.agg.c) <- data.cov.agg.c$gene_cluster_id
data.cov.agg.c <- data.cov.agg.c[,-1]
data.cov.agg.c.comm <- as.data.frame(t(data.cov.agg.c))
data.cov.agg.c.comm <- round(data.cov.agg.c.comm , digits=0)
data.cov.agg.c.comm <- data.cov.agg.c.comm [, which(colSums(data.cov.agg.c.comm ) != 0)] # 22,190 accessory gene clusters with non zero coverage values (same as method round then aggregate)
sum(data.cov.agg.c.comm) # 7,468,898 total GC counts
```

Linear model and plot
```{r}
summary(lm(rowSums(data.cov.agg.c.comm) ~ reads.data$mapped_reads))
summary(lm(rowSums(data.cov.agg.c.comm) ~ rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)))
```
Again, the correlation between total number of GC count (hits) and the total number of mapped reads per sample is almost perfect (R2=99.89%).
The correlation between the counts of the two methods are exactly the same!! (R2 = 1)


### Conclusion 

Normally, rarefaction would directly take place at the level of metagenomic sequences or at the number of reads mapped to Methylobacterium genomes, by sample. Since it is more difficult to implement this in my anvio pipeline, I will take another approach and rarefy at the level of gene cluster 'hits', since GC counts are highly correlated with number of reads mapped

A gene or GC 'hit' is defined as a rounded coverage value of 1. 
The total number of 'hits' for a given GC is thus defined as the sum of rounded gene calls' (clustered to the given GC) coverage.

GC hits will represent this GC's abundance.

I will rarefy so that every sample have the same total number of 'hits' to any gene cluster. This is ok since, as my analyses above show, there is an almost perfect correlation between total number of hits to any GC and total number of metagenomic reads mapped to Methylobacterium genomes. Rounding coverage values before or after aggregating (sum) do not change the correlation strength (R2), and almost do not change the total number of GC counts. I will round, then aggregate after, to avoid a gene cluster being included just because its sum of very small value (eg. coverages of 0.1) is >1.

```{r}
rm(temp1, data.cov.agg.c, data.cov.agg.c.comm)
```


   NO NEED TO RERUN CHUNKS ABOVE (TESTS ONLY)
   ##########################################


################################################################################

# TESTS : Rarefying exploration


   ##########################################
   NO NEED TO RERUN CHUNKS BELOW (TESTS ONLY)

## Gene calls levels : Exploration of rarefying at the gene call level

Prepare data
```{r}
data.cov.c.comm <- t(data.cov.c)
data.cov.c.comm <- as.data.frame(data.cov.c.comm)
```

Rarefying (« rarefying », sensu Schloss 2024, i.e. one random subsampling set at the lowest number of total coverage counts) 
```{r}
rowSums(data.cov.c.comm)
data.cov.c.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.comm[rowSums(data.cov.c.comm) >= 91347,]
rowSums(data.cov.c.comm.rar)
data.cov.c.comm.rar <- rrarefy(data.cov.c.comm.rar, sample = 91347)
data.cov.c.comm.rar <- as.data.frame(data.cov.c.comm.rar)
rowSums(data.cov.c.comm.rar)
rarecurve(data.cov.c.comm.rar, step=200, label=FALSE, xlim=c(0,100000))
# Stats post-rarefaction
data.cov.c.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.comm.rar[,colSums(data.cov.c.comm.rar)>0]
ncol(data.cov.c.comm.rar)/ncol(data.cov.c.comm) # Loss of 80% of gene calls, and 5 samples. 
```
### Conclusion: I cannot rarefy at the gene call levels, because I lost too much samples and too much gene calls in any cases.

```{r}
rm(data.cov.c.comm.rar, data.cov.c.comm)
```

   NO NEED TO RERUN CHUNKS ABOVE (TESTS ONLY)
   ##########################################

# TESTS : Rarefying tests


   ##########################################
   NO NEED TO RERUN CHUNKS BELOW (TESTS ONLY)


## Curves
```{r}
rarecurve(data.cov.c.agg.comm, step = 1000, label = F, xlim=c(0,max(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm))))
abline(v = 44221, col = "red")
abline(v = 47322, col = "firebrick")
abline(v = 66008, col = "darkorange")
abline(v = 91347, col = "yellow")
```

```{r}
rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)
rarecurve(data.cov.c.agg.comm, step = 1000, label = F, xlim=c(0,100000), ylim=c(0,12500))
abline(v = 23574, col = "purple")
abline(v = 44221, col = "red")
abline(v = 47322, col = "firebrick")
abline(v = 66008, col = "darkorange")
abline(v = 91347, col = "yellow")
```

Number of total GC counts per sample
```{r}
rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)
plot(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm), xaxt = 'n')
axis(1, at = 1:nrow(data.cov.c.agg.comm), labels = rownames(data.cov.c.agg.comm), las = 2)
abline(h = 44221, col = "red")
abline(h = 47322, col = "firebrick")
abline(h = 66008, col = "darkorange")
abline(h = 91347, col = "yellow")
abline(h = mean(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)), col = "blue")
```

## Tests

Rarefying at 12004
```{r}
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm[which(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)>=12004),]
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- as.data.frame(rrarefy(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar, sample = 12004))
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar[,which(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)!=0)] # 14,392 gene clusters left after rarefaction 
ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)-ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar) # loss of 7798 GC after rarefaction
1-(ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)/ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)) # loss of 35.14% of GC
# no loss of samples 
```

Rarefying at 23574
```{r}
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm[which(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)>=23574),]
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- as.data.frame(rrarefy(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar, sample = 23574))
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar[,which(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)!=0)] # 16,024 gene clusters left after rarefaction 
ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)-ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar) # loss of 6166 GC after rarefaction
1-(ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)/ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)) # loss of 27.79% of GC
# loss of 1 sample
```

Rarefying at 44221
```{r}
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm[which(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)>=44221),]
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- as.data.frame(rrarefy(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar, sample = 44221))
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar[,which(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)!=0)] # 17,467 gene clusters left after rarefaction 
ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)-ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar) # loss of 4723 GC after rarefaction 
1-(ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)/ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)) # loss of 21.28% of GC
# loss of 2 samples
```

## Rarefying at 44221 BUT FILTERING LOW ABUNDANT GC BEFORE

Filtering
```{r}
data.cov.c.agg.comm.filt <- data.cov.c.agg.comm[,colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm) >= 0.00001*sum(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm))] # Remove GC that did not make up a total of 0.001% of total GC counts across samples. (i.e. GC that have less than 1 count for every 100,000 counts)

```

Rarefaction curves
```{r}
rarecurve(data.cov.c.agg.comm.filt, step = 1000, label = F, xlim=c(0,max(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm))))
abline(v = 44221, col = "red")
abline(v = 47322, col = "firebrick")
abline(v = 66008, col = "darkorange")
abline(v = 91347, col = "yellow")
```

```{r}
rarecurve(data.cov.c.agg.comm.filt, step = 1000, label = F, xlim=c(0,100000))
abline(v = 44221, col = "red")
abline(v = 47322, col = "firebrick")
abline(v = 66008, col = "darkorange")
abline(v = 91347, col = "yellow")
```
Rarefaction
```{r}
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm[which(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)>=44221),]
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- as.data.frame(rrarefy(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar, sample = 44221))
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar[,which(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)!=0)] # X gene clusters left after rarefaction 
ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)-ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar) # loss of X GC after rarefaction 
1-(ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)/ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)) # loss of X % of GC
# loss of X samples
```


Rarefying at 47322
```{r}
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm[which(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)>=47322),]
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- as.data.frame(rrarefy(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar, sample = 47322))
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar[,which(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)!=0)] # 17,581 gene clusters left after rarefaction 
ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)-ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar) # loss of 4609 GC after rarefaction
1-(ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)/ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)) # loss of 20.77% of GC
# loss of 3 samples
```

Rarefying at 66008
```{r}
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm[which(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)>=66008),]
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- as.data.frame(rrarefy(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar, sample = 66008))
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar[,which(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)!=0)] # 18368 gene clusters left after rarefaction 
ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)-ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar) # loss of 3822 GC after rarefaction
1-(ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)/ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)) # loss of 17.22% of GC
```

Rarefying at 91347
```{r}
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm[which(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)>=91347),]
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- as.data.frame(rrarefy(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar, sample = 91347))
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar[,which(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)!=0)] # 19,045 gene clusters left after rarefaction 
ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)-ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar) # loss of 3145 GC after rarefaction
1-(ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)/ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)) # loss of 14.17% of GC
# loss of 5 samples
```

If I rarefy more, I will lose one more ACSA sample and will only have two left (not enough for stats).
I cannot rarefy higher.

### Conclusion
Rarefying at 91347 is keeping enough sample of every host species for stats analyses while only getting rid of rare GC. THIS IS WHAT ID DID IN V3 OF THE SCRIPT.

HOWEVER, EVEN IF I KEEP MORE GC, I NEED STATISTICAL POWER TO DETECT THE DIFFERENCE IN ABUNDANCE OF THOSE GC. 

* MOST IMPORTANTLY
At this low number of samples per host species, chance is playing in role in detecting genes. So the more I add samples, the more I gain precision on the biological reality.
Adding samples results in having fewer DAGs (verification done), because often the added sample do not contain a GC that is abundant in the other samples.

SO IN SCRIPT V4 and V5, I WILL RAREFY AT 44221.

If I rarefy lower, the curves are still steep so I would lose too much information.



   NO NEED TO RERUN CHUNKS ABOVE (TESTS ONLY)

# Rarefying run

```{r}
rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)
metadata$GC.counts.pre.rar <- rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)
```

Plot
```{r}
ggplot(metadata, aes(x = host_species, y = GC.counts.pre.rar)) +
geom_boxplot(alpha = 1, aes(fill = host_species), show.legend = FALSE, outlier.shape = NA)+
geom_point(aes(color = host_species), 
           position = position_jitter(0.075), 
           shape = 16, 
           size = 3, show.legend = FALSE)+
scale_fill_manual(values=c("#63AFF9","#1EA670","#CA0E78","#F39635","#6104BB"))+
scale_color_manual(values=c("black","black","black","black","black"))+
labs(x = "Host Species", 
     y = "Gene cluster counts before rarefying")+
scale_x_discrete(labels = c("ABBA" = "A. balsamea", "THOC" = "T. occidentalis","ACSA" = "A. Saccharum", "FAGR" = "F. grandifolia", "COCO" = "C. cornuta"))+
#ylim(0,25) +
theme_bw()+
theme(text = element_text(family = "Arial"),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 11, angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0, face = "italic"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 11, angle = 0, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5),  
      axis.title.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 13, angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, vjust = -1, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 13, angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 2, face = "bold")
      )
ggsave("../figures/GC_counts_pre_rarefaction.tiff", width = 6.5, height = 4.3333)
```
ANOVA
```{r}
lm <- lm(GC.counts.pre.rar ~ host_species, metadata)
summary(lm)
anova(lm)
plot(lm)
hist(residuals(lm), breaks =25)
shapiro.test(residuals(lm))
```


Rarefying at 44,221
```{r}
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm[which(rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm) >= 44221),]
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- as.data.frame(rrarefy(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar, sample = 44221))
```

Save 'data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar' as RDS file for consistency in rerunning the code.
```{r}
#saveRDS(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar, "../rds/data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar_v5.rds")
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- readRDS("../rds/data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar_v5.rds")
```

## Adjust metadata table
```{r}
metadata.rar <- metadata[rownames(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar),]
#saveRDS(metadata.rar, "../rds/metadata.rar.rds")
```

Check the effect of rarefying
```{r}
# Statistics
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar <- data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar[,which(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)!=0)] # 17,488 gene clusters left after rarefaction 
ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)-ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar) # loss of 4702 GC after rarefaction
1-(ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)/ncol(data.cov.c.agg.comm)) # loss of 21.19% of GC
# loss of 2 samples (1 ACSA, 1 FAGR)

# rarefied vs. raw GC richness
rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)
richness_raw <- rowSums((data.cov.c.agg.comm[rownames(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar),]>0)*1)
richness_rar <- rowSums((data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar>0)*1)
plot(richness_rar~richness_raw,xlab='number of GC in raw data',ylab='number of GC in rarefied data') + abline(0,1)
rm(richness_raw,richness_rar)
```
There is quite a loss of GC when rarefaction is applied. But these GC are relatively rare. The benefits of rarefaction for beta diversity analyses and DAGs analyses accuracy overweight the loss of rare GC.

## GC lost in rarefying
```{r}
# Create and prep dataframe
lost.GC <- data.cov.c.agg.comm[, setdiff(colnames(data.cov.c.agg.comm), colnames(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar))]

max(colSums(lost.GC))
mean(colSums(lost.GC))
median(colSums(lost.GC))
min(colSums(lost.GC))

max(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar))
mean(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar))
median(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar))
min(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar))

lost.GC.sums <- data.frame(GC = colnames(lost.GC), sum = colSums(lost.GC), keep = as.factor(rep("lost")))

kept.GC.sums <- data.frame(GC = colnames(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar), sum = colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar), keep = as.factor(rep("kept")))

GC.c <- rbind(lost.GC.sums, kept.GC.sums)

# Plot exploration
plot(sum ~ keep, GC.c, ylim = c(0, 50))

ggplot(GC.c, aes(x = keep, y = sum)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "blue", color = "black")

ggplot(GC.c, aes(x = keep, y = sum), fill = keep) +
  geom_violin(size = 1, bw = 0.5, color = "black") +
  ylim(0,50)

hist(GC.c[GC.c$keep == "lost",]$sum, breaks = 50)
hist(GC.c[GC.c$keep == "kept",]$sum, breaks = 1000)

ggplot(GC.c, aes(x = keep, y = log(sum))) +
  geom_violin(fill = "blue", color = "black") +
  ylim(0,10)

hist(log(GC.c[GC.c$keep == "lost",]$sum), breaks = 50)
hist(log(GC.c[GC.c$keep == "kept",]$sum), breaks = 1000)

# Plot
ggplot(GC.c, aes(x = keep, y = sum, fill = keep)) + 
  geom_violin(size = 0.5, bw = 0.2, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("red", "blue")) +  # Customize the fill colors
  ylim(0, 100) + 
  annotate("text", x = 2, y = 95, label = "max = 6999", size = 4, color = "black", hjust = -0.25)
```

## Filtering of rarefied data for analyses (Remove low abundant GC)
```{r}
data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt <- data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar[,colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar) >= 0.00001*sum(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar))] # Remove GC that did not make up a total of 0.001% of total GC counts across samples. (i.e. GC that have less than 1 count for every 100,000 counts)
```

```{r}
length(colnames(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt)) # 10,091 GC
length(colnames(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt))/length(colnames(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar)) # Keep 57.7% of rarefied GC
length(colnames(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt))/length(colnames(data.cov.c.agg.comm)) # Keep 45.5% of all detected GC
sum(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt)/sum(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar) # Keep 97.4% of rarefied counts
sum(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt)/sum(data.cov.c.agg.comm) # Keep 13.3% of total counts
```


## GC lost in rarefying AND FILTERING
```{r}
# Create and prep dataframe

# 'rf' : rarefied and filtered

lost.GC.pre.rf <- data.cov.c.agg.comm[, setdiff(colnames(data.cov.c.agg.comm), colnames(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt))]
kept.GC.pre.rf <- data.cov.c.agg.comm[, colnames(data.cov.c.agg.comm) %in% colnames(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt)]

# pre rarefaction and filtering
max(colSums(lost.GC.pre.rf))
mean(colSums(lost.GC.pre.rf))
median(colSums(lost.GC.pre.rf))
min(colSums(lost.GC.pre.rf))

# pre rarefaction and filtering
max(colSums(kept.GC.pre.rf))
mean(colSums(kept.GC.pre.rf))
median(colSums(kept.GC.pre.rf))
min(colSums(kept.GC.pre.rf))

# post rarefaction and filtering
max(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt))
mean(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt))
median(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt))
min(colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt))

lost.GC.sums.pre.rf <- data.frame(GC = colnames(lost.GC.pre.rf), sum = colSums(lost.GC.pre.rf), keep = as.factor(rep("lost.pre.rf")))
kept.GC.sums.pre.rf <- data.frame(GC = colnames(kept.GC.pre.rf), sum = colSums(kept.GC.pre.rf), keep = as.factor(rep("kept.pre.rf")))
kept.GC.sums.post.rf <- data.frame(GC = colnames(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt), sum = colSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt), keep = as.factor(rep("kept.post.rf")))
GC.c.rf <- rbind(lost.GC.sums.pre.rf, kept.GC.sums.pre.rf, kept.GC.sums.post.rf)

# Plot exploration
#hist(log(GC.c.rf[GC.c.rf$keep == "lost",]$sum), breaks = 50)
#hist(log(GC.c.rf[GC.c.rf$keep == "kept",]$sum), breaks = 1000)

# Plot
ggplot(GC.c.rf, aes(x = keep, y = sum, fill = keep)) + 
  geom_violin(size = 0.1, bw = 0.2, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("darkorange", "forestgreen", "darkblue")) +
  ylim(0, 200) + 
  annotate("text", x = 1, y = 190, label = "max = 291", size = 4, color = "black", hjust = -0.25) +
  annotate("text", x = 2, y = 190, label = "max = 28,996", size = 4, color = "black", hjust = -0.25) +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = 190, label = "max = 6,999", size = 4, color = "black", hjust = -0.25)
```


################################################################################

# Create dataframe with a simpler name for 'data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt' for subsequent alpha diversity and DAGs analyses. (beta diversity analyses will use rarafactions results of 'avgdist' function)
```{r}
GC.rar.filt <- data.cov.c.agg.comm.rar.filt
#saveRDS(GC.rar.filt, "../rds/GC.rar.filt.rds")
```


################################################################################


# Alpha diversity

## Calculate GC richness and shannon index
```{r}
metadata.rar$richness.GC <- rowSums(GC.rar.filt>0)
metadata.rar$shannon.GC <- vegan::diversity(GC.rar.filt, index = "shannon")
```

## Richness
```{r}
lm <- lm(richness.GC ~ host_species, data = metadata.rar)
plot(richness.GC ~ host_species, data = metadata.rar)
summary(lm)
anova(lm)
plot(lm)
hist(residuals(lm), breaks = 25)
shapiro.test(residuals(lm))
```
No differences in GC richness among host species.

## Shannon index
```{r}
lm <- lm(shannon.GC ~ host_species, data = metadata.rar)
plot(shannon.GC ~ host_species, data = metadata.rar)
summary(lm)
anova(lm)
plot(lm)
hist(log(residuals(lm)), breaks = 50)
shapiro.test(log(residuals(lm)))
# residuals are non normal

# Kruskall-Wallis
kruskal.test(shannon.GC ~ host_species, data = metadata.rar)
rstatix::kruskal_effsize(shannon.GC ~ host_species, data = metadata.rar)
```


################################################################################

# Beta diversity

## NO rarefaction (raw GC counts)
 
```{r}
GC.dist.raw <- vegdist(data.cov.c.agg.comm, method = "bray")
```

### PERMANOVA
```{r}
GC.perm.raw <- adonis2(GC.dist.raw ~ host_species, data = metadata)
GC.perm.raw
```

### BETADISPER
```{r}
GC.disper.raw <- betadisper(GC.dist.raw, metadata$host_species)
anova(GC.disper.raw)
plot(GC.disper.raw)
```


## WITH Rarefaction

## With rarefying + filtering (steps above)

```{r}
GC.rar.filt <- readRDS("../rds/GC.rar.filt.rds")
```

```{r}
GC.dist.rar.filt <- vegdist(GC.rar.filt, method = "bray")
```

### PERMANOVA
```{r}
GC.perm.rar.filt <- adonis2(GC.dist.rar.filt ~ host_species, data = metadata.rar)
GC.perm.rar.filt
```

### BETADISPER
```{r}
GC.disper.rar.filt <- betadisper(GC.dist.rar.filt, metadata.rar$host_species)
anova(GC.disper.rar.filt)
plot(GC.disper.rar.filt)
```

### Test of beta diversity partioning
```{r}
GC.cov.beta <- adespatial::beta.div.comp(GC.rar.filt, coef = "BS", quant = TRUE)
GC.cov.beta[["part"]]
GC.bray.dist <- GC.cov.beta[["D"]]
GC.bray <- data.frame(as.matrix(GC.cov.beta[["D"]]))

GC.cov.beta.host <- data.frame(GC.cov.beta[["part"]])
colnames(GC.cov.beta.host) <- "All samples"
GC.cov.beta.ABBA <- adespatial::beta.div.comp(GC.rar.filt[c(1:5),], coef = "BS", quant = TRUE)
GC.cov.beta.host$ABBA <- GC.cov.beta.ABBA[["part"]]
GC.cov.beta.ACSA <- adespatial::beta.div.comp(GC.rar.filt[c(6:9),], coef = "BS", quant = TRUE)
GC.cov.beta.host$ACSA <- GC.cov.beta.ACSA[["part"]]
GC.cov.beta.COCO <- adespatial::beta.div.comp(GC.rar.filt[c(10:14),], coef = "BS", quant = TRUE)
GC.cov.beta.host$COCO <- GC.cov.beta.COCO[["part"]]
GC.cov.beta.FAGR <- adespatial::beta.div.comp(GC.rar.filt[c(15:18),], coef = "BS", quant = TRUE)
GC.cov.beta.host$FAGR <- GC.cov.beta.FAGR[["part"]]
GC.cov.beta.THOC <- adespatial::beta.div.comp(GC.rar.filt[c(19:23),], coef = "BS", quant = TRUE)
GC.cov.beta.host$THOC <- GC.cov.beta.THOC[["part"]]
```

```{r}
rowSums(data.cov.c.agg.comm)
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
GC.dist.rar <- avgdist(data.cov.c.agg.comm, sample = 44221, dmethod = "bray", iteration = 100)
#saveRDS(GC.dist.rar, "../rds/GC.dist.rar_44221_with_100_iterations_v5.rds")
```

```{r}
GC.dist.rar <- readRDS("../rds/GC.dist.rar_44221_with_100_iterations_v5.rds")
```

Make sure metadata.rar have been created correctly!

### PERMANOVA
```{r}
GC.perm <- adonis2(GC.dist.rar ~ host_species, data = metadata.rar)
GC.perm
```

### BETADISPER
```{r}
GC.disper <- betadisper(GC.dist.rar, metadata.rar$host_species)
anova(GC.disper)
plot(GC.disper)
```

## Pairwise PERMANOVAs
```{r}
shrub_tree_PERM <- adonis2(GC.dist.rar ~ shrub_tree, data = metadata.rar)
shrub_tree_PERM
shrub_tree_DISP <- betadisper(GC.dist.rar, metadata.rar$shrub_tree)
anova(shrub_tree_DISP)
plot(shrub_tree_DISP)

GC.dist.rar.noCOCO <- data.frame(as.matrix(GC.dist.rar))
GC.dist.rar.noCOCO <- GC.dist.rar.noCOCO[c(1:9,15:23),c(1:9,15:23)]
GC.dist.rar.noCOCO <- as.dist(as.matrix(GC.dist.rar.noCOCO), diag = FALSE, upper = FALSE)
gymno_angio_tree_PERM <- adonis2(GC.dist.rar.noCOCO ~ gymno_angio_tree, data = metadata.rar[c(1:9,15:23),])
gymno_angio_tree_PERM
gymno_angio_tree_DISP <- betadisper(GC.dist.rar.noCOCO, metadata.rar[c(1:9,15:23),]$gymno_angio_tree)
anova(gymno_angio_tree_DISP)
plot(gymno_angio_tree_DISP, label = F)
```

I can be confident in my rarefaction for beta diversity analyses, because the results with non rarefied data is quite similar.


### PCoA

#### Axis 1 & 2

```{r}
GC.pcoa <- cmdscale(GC.dist.rar, k = (nrow(metadata.rar) - 1), eig = TRUE)
pcoa.eig <- eigenvals(GC.pcoa) # extract eigenvalues
pcoa.eig <- abs(pcoa.eig) # absolute values
sum.pcoa.eig <- sum(pcoa.eig) # total of eigenvalues
pcoa.prop.exp <- pcoa.eig/sum.pcoa.eig # proportion explained by each axis
(pcoa.axis1 <- pcoa.prop.exp[1])
(pcoa.axis2 <- pcoa.prop.exp[2])
site.scores.pcoa <- scores(GC.pcoa) # site scores
sp.scores.pcoa <- wascores(GC.pcoa$points, data.cov.c.agg.comm[c(1:5, 6:7,9:10, 11:15, 16:17,19:20, 21:25),]) # species scores 
site.scores.pcoa <- data.frame(site.scores.pcoa)
sp.scores.pcoa <- data.frame(sp.scores.pcoa)
host_species <- metadata.rar$host_species
site.scores.pcoa <- cbind(site.scores.pcoa, host_species)
pcoa.ggordiplot <- gg_ordiplot(GC.pcoa, choices = c(1,2), groups = metadata.rar$host_species, kind = "sd", conf = 0.95, pt.size = 3)
pcoa.ellipses <- pcoa.ggordiplot$df_ellipse # extract confidence ellipse
#saveRDS(site.scores.pcoa, "../rds/site.scores.pcoa.rds")
```

Plot
```{r}
ggplot(data = site.scores.pcoa, 
         aes(x = Dim1, y = Dim2), 
         color = host_species) + 
  expand_limits(x=c(-0.2,0.4), y=c(-0.2,0.3)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = host_species, color = host_species), show.legend = T, 
             size=3) +
  xlab(paste("PCoA1 (",round(pcoa.axis1*100, digits = 2),"%)", sep = "")) +
  ylab(paste("PCoA2 (",round(pcoa.axis2*100, digits = 2),"%)", sep = "")) +
  #geom_text_repel(data = sp.scores.pcoa, 
  #                aes(x = X1, y = X2), 
  #                label=rownames(sp.scores.pcoa), 
  #                color = "black") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16,16,16,16,16)) +
  guides(shape = "none") +
  geom_path(data = pcoa.ellipses, 
            aes(x = x, y = y, color = Group), 
            show.legend = FALSE) + 
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#63AFF9","#1EA670","#CA0E78","#F39635","#6104BB"), name = "Host species", labels = c("ABBA" = expression(italic("A. balsamea")), "THOC" = expression(italic("T. occidentalis")),"ACSA" = expression(italic("A. Saccharum")), "FAGR" = expression(italic("F. grandifolia")), "COCO" = expression(italic("C. cornuta")))) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Arial"),
      legend.justification = c(0.5, 0),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.25), 
      #panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "lightgray", size = 0.25), 
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 11, angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0),
      axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 11, angle = 0, hjust = 0, vjust = 0.5),  
      axis.title.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 13, angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, vjust = -1, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 13, angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 2, face = "bold"),
      plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
      )
ggsave("../figures/gene_clusters_pcoa_axis-1-2.tiff", width = 6, height = 4)
```

#### Axis 1 & 3

```{r}
GC.pcoa <- cmdscale(GC.dist.rar, k = (nrow(metadata.rar) - 1), eig = TRUE)
pcoa.eig <- eigenvals(GC.pcoa) # extract eigenvalues
pcoa.eig <- abs(pcoa.eig) # absolute values
sum.pcoa.eig <- sum(pcoa.eig) # total of eigenvalues
pcoa.prop.exp <- pcoa.eig/sum.pcoa.eig # proportion explained by each axis
(pcoa.axis1 <- pcoa.prop.exp[1])
(pcoa.axis3 <- pcoa.prop.exp[3])
site.scores.pcoa <- scores(GC.pcoa) # site scores
sp.scores.pcoa <- wascores(GC.pcoa$points, data.cov.c.agg.comm[c(1:5, 6:7,9:10, 11:15, 16:17,19:20, 21:25),]) # species scores 
site.scores.pcoa <- data.frame(site.scores.pcoa)
sp.scores.pcoa <- data.frame(sp.scores.pcoa)
host_species <- metadata.rar$host_species
site.scores.pcoa <- cbind(site.scores.pcoa, host_species)
pcoa.ggordiplot <- gg_ordiplot(GC.pcoa, choices = c(1,3), groups = metadata.rar$host_species, kind = "sd", conf = 0.95, pt.size = 3)
pcoa.ellipses <- pcoa.ggordiplot$df_ellipse # extract confidence ellipse
```

```{r}
  geom_point(aes(shape = host_species, color = host_species), show.legend = T, 
             size=3) +
  xlab(paste("PCoA1 (",round(pcoa.axis1*100, digits = 2),"%)", sep = "")) +
  ylab(paste("PCoA3 (",round(pcoa.axis3*100, digits = 2),"%)", sep = "")) +
  #geom_text_repel(data = sp.scores.pcoa,

  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16,16,16,16,16)) +
  guides(shape = "none") +
  geom_path(data = pcoa.ellipses, 
            aes(x = x, y = y, color = Group), 
            show.legend = FALSE) + 
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#63AFF9","#1EA670","#CA0E78","#F39635","#6104BB"), name = "Host species", labels = c("ABBA" = expression(italic("A. balsamea")), "THOC" = expression(italic("T. occidentalis")),"ACSA" = expression(italic("A. Saccharum")), "FAGR" = expression(italic("F. grandifolia")), "COCO" = expression(italic("C. cornuta")))) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Arial"),
      legend.justification = c(0.5, 0),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.25), 
      #panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "lightgray", size = 0.25), 
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 11, angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0),
      axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 11, angle = 0, hjust = 0, vjust = 0.5),

      axis.title.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 13, angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 2, face = "bold")
      )
ggsave("../figures/gene_clusters_pcoa_axis-1-3.tiff", width = 6, height = 4)
```

### dbRDA 

Correlation between axes and plant traits

Import plant traits data (must have ran script 'plant_traits_v2.Rmd' before)
```{r}
traits.comm <- readRDS("../rds/traits.comm.rds")
```

Merge traits data with metadata
```{r}
metadata.rar.traits <- merge(metadata.rar, traits.comm, by = "host_species")
```

Import PCoA sites scores
```{r}
site.scores.pcoa <- readRDS("../rds/site.scores.pcoa.rds")
```

Multiple regressions
```{r}
# Incorporate PCoA axes scores in metadata
metadata.rar.traits$PCoA1 <- site.scores.pcoa$Dim1
metadata.rar.traits$PCoA2 <- site.scores.pcoa$Dim2
metadata.rar.traits$PCoA3 <- site.scores.pcoa$Dim3

#Normalize (Scaling and Centering) traits values and PCoA scores
metadata.rar.traits[,c(13:20)] <- scale(metadata.rar.traits[,c(13:20)])

# PCoA Axis 1
lm1 <- lm(PCoA1 ~ P, metadata.rar.traits)
summary(lm1)
car::Anova(lm1, type = "2")
plot(PCoA1 ~ P, metadata.rar.traits)
hist(residuals(lm1), breaks = 25)
shapiro.test(residuals(lm1))

lm1 <- lm(PCoA1 ~ N, metadata.rar.traits)
summary(lm1)
car::Anova(lm1, type = "2")
plot(PCoA1 ~ P, metadata.rar.traits)
hist(residuals(lm1), breaks = 25)
shapiro.test(residuals(lm1))

lm1 <- lm(PCoA1 ~ LDMC, metadata.rar.traits)
summary(lm1)
car::Anova(lm1, type = "2")
plot(PCoA1 ~ P, metadata.rar.traits)
hist(residuals(lm1), breaks = 25)
shapiro.test(residuals(lm1))

# PCoA Axis 2
lm2 <- lm(PCoA2 ~ P + N + LDMC, metadata.rar.traits)
summary(lm2)
car::Anova(lm2, type = "2")
plot(PCoA2 ~ N, metadata.rar.traits)
hist(residuals(lm2), breaks = 25)
shapiro.test(residuals(lm2))

# PCoA Axis 3
lm3 <- lm(PCoA3 ~ P + N + LDMC, metadata.rar.traits)
summary(lm3)
car::Anova(lm3, type = "3")
plot(PCoA3 ~ N, metadata.rar.traits)
hist(residuals(lm3), breaks = 35)
shapiro.test(residuals(lm3))
```

dbRDA
```{r}
dbrda <- dbrda(GC.dist.rar ~ P + N + LDMC, data = metadata.rar.traits)
summary(dbrda)
plot(dbrda)
anova.cca(dbrda, by = "margin", permutations = 999)
RsquareAdj(dbrda)
```

Extract proportions explained by axes 1 and 2
```{r}
dbrda.eig <- eigenvals(dbrda, model = "constrained") # extract eigenvalues
dbrda.eig <- abs(dbrda.eig) # calculate eigenvalues absolute values
dbrda.eig.sum <- sum(dbrda.eig) # total of all eigenvalues
dbrda.prop.exp <- dbrda.eig/dbrda.eig.sum # proportion explained by every axes
(dbrda.axis1 <- dbrda.prop.exp[1])
(dbrda.axis2 <- dbrda.prop.exp[2])
```

Extract dbRDA scores
```{r}
dbrda.site.scores <- data.frame(scores(dbrda, display = "sites", scaling = "sites")) # site scores
host_species <- metadata.rar.traits$host_species
dbrda.site.scores <- cbind(dbrda.site.scores,host_species) # Add host_species factor in site scores data frame for ggplot
rm(host_species)

#sppscores(dbrda) <- GC.agg.acc.count.rar
#dbrda.sp.scores <- data.frame(scores(dbrda, display = "species", scaling = "sites")) # species scores 
#dbrda.sp.scores.DA <- dbrda.sp.scores[rownames(DA.KM),] # Keep only KEGG Module which are differentially abundant in DESeq2 analysis
```


Prepare arrows data (leaf traits)
```{r}
biplot_data <- scores(dbrda, display = "bp", scaling = "sites")
anova.res <- anova.cca(dbrda, by = "margin", permutations = 999)
#saveRDS(anova.res, "../rds/dbrda_traits_anova_res.rds")
anova.res <- readRDS("../rds/dbrda_traits_anova_res.rds")
sig.traits <- anova.res[anova.res$`Pr(>F)` < 0.05, ]
sig.biplot_data <- as.data.frame(biplot_data[rownames(biplot_data) %in% rownames(sig.traits), ])
```


dbRDA plot
```{r}
ggplot(data = dbrda.site.scores, 
         aes(x = dbRDA1, y = dbRDA2), 
         color = host_species) + 
  expand_limits(x=c(-0.45,0.4), y=c(-0.32,0.2)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = host_species, color = host_species), show.legend = T, 
             size=3) +
  xlab("dbRDA1 (55.76%)") + 
  ylab("dbRDA2 (26.13%)") +
  geom_segment(data = sig.biplot_data,
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = dbRDA1, yend = dbRDA2),
               arrow = arrow(type = "closed", length = unit(0.1, "inches")),
               size = 0.5, color = "black") +
  geom_text(data = sig.biplot_data,
            aes(x = dbRDA1, y = dbRDA2, label = rownames(sig.biplot_data)),
            vjust = 1.75, hjust = 0, color = "black", size = 5) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16,16,16,16,16)) +
  guides(shape = "none") +
  #geom_path(data = pcoa1.ellipses, 
  #          aes(x = x, y = y, color = Group), 
  #          show.legend = FALSE) + 
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#63AFF9","#1EA670","#CA0E78","#F39635","#6104BB"), name = "Host species", labels = c("ABBA" = expression(italic("A. balsamea")), "THOC" = expression(italic("T. occidentalis")),"ACSA" = expression(italic("A. Saccharum")), "FAGR" = expression(italic("F. grandifolia")), "COCO" = expression(italic("C. cornuta")))) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Arial"),
      legend.justification = c(0.5, 0),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.25), 
      #panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "lightgray", size = 0.25), 
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 11, angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0),
      axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 11, angle = 0, hjust = 0, vjust = 0.5),  
      axis.title.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 13, angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, vjust = -1, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 13, angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 2, face = "bold"),
      plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
      )
ggsave("../figures/dbrda_traits.tiff", width = 6, height = 4)
```


################################################################################

# DAGs TAKE 1 - (FOR MEMOIRE, I TOOK TAKE 2's approach)

Analyses ran with rarefied data, because library size (number of total GC hits) was much higher for COCO samples.

For justification :
Weiss, S., Xu, Z. Z., Peddada, S., Amir, A., Bittinger, K., Gonzalez, A., ... & Knight, R. (2017). Normalization and microbial differential abundance strategies depend upon data characteristics. Microbiome, 5, 1-18.

```{r}
options(scipen = 999)
```


## Prepare data

## Rarefied and filtered data

Use data after 'rarefying' at 44,221 and after filtering low abundant GC

```{r}
countData <- as.data.frame(t(GC.rar.filt))
sum(rowSums(countData)==0)
colSums(countData)
```

Make sure count dataframe's columns and metadata's rows are in the same order
```{r}
all(rownames(metadata.rar) == colnames(countData))
```

Analysis
```{r}
GC.dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = countData,
                              colData = metadata.rar, 
                              design = ~ host_species)
GC.dds$host_species <- relevel(GC.dds$host_species, ref = "COCO") # Make COCO reference level
GC.dds <- DESeq(GC.dds)
resultsNames(GC.dds) # lists the coefficients
```

Save and load GC.dds as RDS file
```{r}
#saveRDS(GC.dds, "../rds/GC.dds_v5.rds")
GC.dds <- readRDS("../rds/GC.dds_v5.rds")
```


Exploring the results
```{r}
GC.res.ABBA.ACSA <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","ABBA","ACSA"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.ABBA.COCO <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","ABBA","COCO"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.ABBA.FAGR <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","ABBA","FAGR"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.ABBA.THOC <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","ABBA","THOC"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.ACSA.COCO <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","ACSA","COCO"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.ACSA.FAGR <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","ACSA","FAGR"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.ACSA.THOC <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","ACSA","THOC"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.COCO.FAGR <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","COCO","FAGR"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.COCO.THOC <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","COCO","THOC"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.FAGR.THOC <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","FAGR","THOC"), alpha = 0.05)
```
Exploring (continue...) GC with signicant differential abundances between host tree species (adjusted_p_value<0.05)
```{r}
GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA <- GC.res.ABBA.ACSA[is.na(GC.res.ABBA.ACSA$padj)==F&GC.res.ABBA.ACSA$padj<0.05,] # 52 DAGs
GC.sig.ABBA.COCO <- GC.res.ABBA.COCO[is.na(GC.res.ABBA.COCO$padj)==F&GC.res.ABBA.COCO$padj<0.05,] # 352
GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR <- GC.res.ABBA.FAGR[is.na(GC.res.ABBA.FAGR$padj)==F&GC.res.ABBA.FAGR$padj<0.05,] # 3
GC.sig.ABBA.THOC <- GC.res.ABBA.THOC[is.na(GC.res.ABBA.THOC$padj)==F&GC.res.ABBA.THOC$padj<0.05,] # 0
GC.sig.ACSA.COCO <- GC.res.ACSA.COCO[is.na(GC.res.ACSA.COCO$padj)==F&GC.res.ACSA.COCO$padj<0.05,] # 125
GC.sig.ACSA.FAGR <- GC.res.ACSA.FAGR[is.na(GC.res.ACSA.FAGR$padj)==F&GC.res.ACSA.FAGR$padj<0.05,] # 0
GC.sig.ACSA.THOC <- GC.res.ACSA.THOC[is.na(GC.res.ACSA.THOC$padj)==F&GC.res.ACSA.THOC$padj<0.05,] # 16
GC.sig.COCO.FAGR <- GC.res.COCO.FAGR[is.na(GC.res.COCO.FAGR$padj)==F&GC.res.COCO.FAGR$padj<0.05,] # 8
GC.sig.COCO.THOC <- GC.res.COCO.THOC[is.na(GC.res.COCO.THOC$padj)==F&GC.res.COCO.THOC$padj<0.05,] # 233
GC.sig.FAGR.THOC <- GC.res.FAGR.THOC[is.na(GC.res.FAGR.THOC$padj)==F&GC.res.FAGR.THOC$padj<0.05,] # 1
```
Observation
There is more DAGs between COCO and conifer trees than between COCO and angiosperm trees
There is more DAGs between COCO and ACSA, than between COCO and FAGR
There is more DAGs between ACSA and conifers than between FAGR and conifers
There is no DAGs between angiosperm tree species.
There is no DAGs between conifer tree species.

OVERALL, whether we look at bacterial genera communities, Methylobacterium species communities, or Methylobacterium gene communities, the main pattern of differences is always between COCO and the rest (sometimes COCO is closer to angiosperm trees, but not always) and between angiosperm and gymnosperm trees.

Remove GC.sig. and GC.res. objects
```{r}
rm(list = ls(pattern = "^GC\\.sig\\."))
rm(list = ls(pattern = "^GC\\.res\\."))
```


## Prepare dataframe containing rarefied and filtered GC and their (rarefied) coverage values, and functional annotations.
```{r}
# Edit rarefied and filtered GC dataframe
GC.rar.filt.t <- as.data.frame(t(GC.rar.filt))
GC.rar.filt.t$gene_cluster_id <- rownames(GC.rar.filt.t)
row.names(GC.rar.filt.t) <- NULL

# Edit 'data.fct.cov.c' to remove coverage values
data.fct <- data.fct.cov.c[,c(1:41)]

# Merge dataframes to have GC that were kept after rarefaction and filtering, with their coverage values (rarefied values)
GC.rar.fct.cov <- merge(data.fct, GC.rar.filt.t, by = "gene_cluster_id")
GC.rar.fct.cov <- GC.rar.fct.cov[order(GC.rar.fct.cov$gene_cluster_id, GC.rar.fct.cov$genome_name), ]
GC.rar.fct.cov <- droplevels(GC.rar.fct.cov)
```


## Build dataframes for DAGs

### Trees vs. COCO
For genes specific to Trees only
Common positives in comparisons :
ABBA - COCO
THOC - COCO
ACSA - COCO
FAGR - COCO

Pairwise DAGs
```{r}
GC.res.ABBA.COCO <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","ABBA","COCO"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.THOC.COCO <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","THOC","COCO"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.ACSA.COCO <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","ACSA","COCO"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.FAGR.COCO <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","FAGR","COCO"), alpha = 0.05)
# Make dataframe for each pairwise comparison ("contrast" results) with significative adj. p values and positive log2FoldChange
GC.sig.ABBA.COCO <- as.data.frame(GC.res.ABBA.COCO[is.na(GC.res.ABBA.COCO$padj)==F&GC.res.ABBA.COCO$padj<0.05&GC.res.ABBA.COCO$log2FoldChange>0,])
GC.sig.THOC.COCO <- as.data.frame(GC.res.THOC.COCO[is.na(GC.res.THOC.COCO$padj)==F&GC.res.THOC.COCO$padj<0.05&GC.res.THOC.COCO$log2FoldChange>0,])
GC.sig.ACSA.COCO <- as.data.frame(GC.res.ACSA.COCO[is.na(GC.res.ACSA.COCO$padj)==F&GC.res.ACSA.COCO$padj<0.05&GC.res.ACSA.COCO$log2FoldChange>0,])
GC.sig.FAGR.COCO <- as.data.frame(GC.res.FAGR.COCO[is.na(GC.res.FAGR.COCO$padj)==F&GC.res.FAGR.COCO$padj<0.05&GC.res.FAGR.COCO$log2FoldChange>0,])
```
Note : COCO also have less DAGs with FAGR than ACSA, could it have a link with COCO and FAGR being in the same order?

Create dataframe with common DAGs (associated with Trees) with mean log2 Fold Change and functions
```{r}
# Arrange the 4 results dataframes in decreasing log2FoldChange
GC.sig.ABBA.COCO$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.ABBA.COCO))
GC.sig.ABBA.COCO <- GC.sig.ABBA.COCO[order(-GC.sig.ABBA.COCO$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.ABBA.COCO) <- NULL

GC.sig.THOC.COCO$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.THOC.COCO))
GC.sig.THOC.COCO <- GC.sig.THOC.COCO[order(-GC.sig.THOC.COCO$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.THOC.COCO) <- NULL

GC.sig.ACSA.COCO$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.ACSA.COCO))
GC.sig.ACSA.COCO <- GC.sig.ACSA.COCO[order(-GC.sig.ACSA.COCO$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.ACSA.COCO) <- NULL

GC.sig.FAGR.COCO$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.FAGR.COCO))
GC.sig.FAGR.COCO <- GC.sig.FAGR.COCO[order(-GC.sig.FAGR.COCO$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.FAGR.COCO) <- NULL

# List common DAGs in all 4 df
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.ABBA.COCO$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.COCO$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.ACSA.COCO$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.FAGR.COCO$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# NO DAGs

# List common DAGs in ABBA+THOC+ACSA vs. COCO
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.ABBA.COCO$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.COCO$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.ACSA.COCO$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# 41 DAGs

# Calculate the mean log2FoldChange of the common DAGs for (ABBA+THOC+ACSA vs. COCO)
GC.df.temp <- rbind(GC.sig.ABBA.COCO, GC.sig.THOC.COCO, GC.sig.ACSA.COCO)
GC.df.temp <- GC.df.temp[GC.df.temp$gene_cluster_id %in% GC.list.common.temp, ]
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO <- aggregate(log2FoldChange ~ gene_cluster_id, data = GC.df.temp, FUN = mean) # Calculate mean log2FoldChange for each common GC
colnames(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO)[2] <- "mean_log2FC"
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO <- GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO[order(-GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO$mean_log2FC), ] # Order GC in decreasing mean log2FoldChange
rm(GC.list.temp, GC.list.common.temp, GC.df.temp) # remove temp objects

# Add comparisons
# MANUAL ENTER, VERIFY IF SOMETHING CHANGE EN AMONT!!
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO$ABBA <- rep(1,nrow(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO))
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO$THOC <- rep(1,nrow(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO))
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO$ACSA <- rep(1,nrow(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO))
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO$FAGR <- rep(0,nrow(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO))
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO$COCO <- rep(-1,nrow(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO))

# Merge DAGs with function and coverage df
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct <- merge(GC.rar.fct.cov, GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO, by = "gene_cluster_id")

# Keep rows with unique functional annotation
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct <- GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct %>%
  distinct(gene_cluster_id, COG20_FUNCTION, KOfam, Pfam, CAZyme, Description, .keep_all = TRUE)
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct <- GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct[,-c(2:3)] # remove gene caller id and genome name because irrelevant
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct <- GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct[c(1:3,63,4:62,64:68)] # rearrange column order
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct <- GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct[order(-GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct$mean_log2FC), ] # place rows in order of decreasing mean_log2FC
row.names(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct) <- NULL
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct <- droplevels(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct)
str(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct)

```

Remove GC.sig. and GC.res. objects
```{r}
rm(list = ls(pattern = "^GC\\.sig\\."))
rm(list = ls(pattern = "^GC\\.res\\."))
```


### COCO vs. Trees
For genes specific to COCO only
Common positives in comparisons :
COCO - ABBA
COCO - THOC
COCO - ACSA
COCO - FAGR

Pairwise DAGs
```{r}
GC.res.COCO.ABBA <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","COCO","ABBA"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.COCO.THOC <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","COCO","THOC"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.COCO.ACSA <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","COCO","ACSA"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.COCO.FAGR <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","COCO","FAGR"), alpha = 0.05)
# Make dataframe for each pairwise comparison ("contrast" results) with significative adj. p values and positive log2FoldChange
GC.sig.COCO.ABBA <- as.data.frame(GC.res.COCO.ABBA[is.na(GC.res.COCO.ABBA$padj)==F&GC.res.COCO.ABBA$padj<0.05&GC.res.COCO.ABBA$log2FoldChange>0,])
GC.sig.COCO.THOC <- as.data.frame(GC.res.COCO.THOC[is.na(GC.res.COCO.THOC$padj)==F&GC.res.COCO.THOC$padj<0.05&GC.res.COCO.THOC$log2FoldChange>0,])
GC.sig.COCO.ACSA <- as.data.frame(GC.res.COCO.ACSA[is.na(GC.res.COCO.ACSA$padj)==F&GC.res.COCO.ACSA$padj<0.05&GC.res.COCO.ACSA$log2FoldChange>0,])
GC.sig.COCO.FAGR <- as.data.frame(GC.res.COCO.FAGR[is.na(GC.res.COCO.FAGR$padj)==F&GC.res.COCO.FAGR$padj<0.05&GC.res.COCO.FAGR$log2FoldChange>0,])
```
Note : COCO also have less DAGs with FAGR than ACSA, could it have a link with COCO and FAGR being in the same order?

Create dataframe with common DAGs (associated with Trees) with mean log2 Fold Change and functions
```{r}
# Arrange the 4 results dataframes in decreasing log2FoldChange
GC.sig.COCO.ABBA$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.COCO.ABBA))
GC.sig.COCO.ABBA <- GC.sig.COCO.ABBA[order(-GC.sig.COCO.ABBA$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.COCO.ABBA) <- NULL

GC.sig.COCO.THOC$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.COCO.THOC))
GC.sig.COCO.THOC <- GC.sig.COCO.THOC[order(-GC.sig.COCO.THOC$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.COCO.THOC) <- NULL

GC.sig.COCO.ACSA$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.COCO.ACSA))
GC.sig.COCO.ACSA <- GC.sig.COCO.ACSA[order(-GC.sig.COCO.ACSA$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.COCO.ACSA) <- NULL

GC.sig.COCO.FAGR$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.COCO.FAGR))
GC.sig.COCO.FAGR <- GC.sig.COCO.FAGR[order(-GC.sig.COCO.FAGR$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.COCO.FAGR) <- NULL

# List common DAGs in all 4 df
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.COCO.ABBA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.COCO.THOC$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.COCO.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.COCO.FAGR$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# 2 DAGs

# Calculate the mean log2FoldChange of the common DAGs
GC.df.temp <- rbind(GC.sig.COCO.ABBA, GC.sig.COCO.THOC, GC.sig.COCO.ACSA, GC.sig.COCO.FAGR)
GC.df.temp <- GC.df.temp[GC.df.temp$gene_cluster_id %in% GC.list.common.temp, ]
GC.COCO.Trees <- aggregate(log2FoldChange ~ gene_cluster_id, data = GC.df.temp, FUN = mean) # Calculate mean log2FoldChange for each common GC
colnames(GC.COCO.Trees)[2] <- "mean_log2FC"
GC.COCO.Trees <- GC.COCO.Trees[order(-GC.COCO.Trees$mean_log2FC), ] # Order GC in decreasing mean log2FoldChange
rm(GC.list.temp, GC.list.common.temp, GC.df.temp) # remove temp objects

# Add comparisons
# MANUAL ENTER, VERIFY IF SOMETHING CHANGE EN AMONT!!
GC.COCO.Trees$ABBA <- rep(-1,nrow(GC.COCO.Trees))
GC.COCO.Trees$THOC <- rep(-1,nrow(GC.COCO.Trees))
GC.COCO.Trees$ACSA <- rep(-1,nrow(GC.COCO.Trees))
GC.COCO.Trees$FAGR <- rep(-1,nrow(GC.COCO.Trees))
GC.COCO.Trees$COCO <- rep(1,nrow(GC.COCO.Trees))

# Merge DAGs with function and coverage df
GC.COCO.Trees.fct <- merge(GC.rar.fct.cov, GC.COCO.Trees, by = "gene_cluster_id")

# Keep rows with unique functional annotation
GC.COCO.Trees.fct.distinct <- GC.COCO.Trees.fct %>%
  distinct(gene_cluster_id, COG20_FUNCTION, KOfam, Pfam, CAZyme, Description, .keep_all = TRUE)
GC.COCO.Trees.fct.distinct <- GC.COCO.Trees.fct.distinct[,-c(2:3)] # remove gene caller id and genome name because irrelevant
GC.COCO.Trees.fct.distinct <- GC.COCO.Trees.fct.distinct[c(1:3,63,4:62,64:68)] # rearrange column order
GC.COCO.Trees.fct.distinct <- GC.COCO.Trees.fct.distinct[order(-GC.COCO.Trees.fct.distinct$mean_log2FC), ] # place rows in order of decreasing mean_log2FC
row.names(GC.COCO.Trees.fct.distinct) <- NULL
GC.COCO.Trees.fct.distinct <- droplevels(GC.COCO.Trees.fct.distinct)
str(GC.COCO.Trees.fct.distinct)


# List common DAGs in COCO vs ABBA+THOC+ACSA
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.COCO.ABBA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.COCO.THOC$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.COCO.ACSA$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# 2 DAGs : the SAME DAGs than the 2 from COCO vs TREES
```

Remove GC.sig. and GC.res. objects
```{r}
rm(list = ls(pattern = "^GC\\.sig\\."))
rm(list = ls(pattern = "^GC\\.res\\."))
```



### Gymnosperm vs angiosperms
For genes specific to gymnosperm trees or gymnosperm species

Pairwise DAGs
```{r}
GC.res.ABBA.ACSA <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","ABBA","ACSA"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.ABBA.FAGR <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","ABBA","FAGR"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.ABBA.COCO <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","ABBA","COCO"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.THOC.ACSA <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","THOC","ACSA"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.THOC.FAGR <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","THOC","FAGR"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.THOC.COCO <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","THOC","COCO"), alpha = 0.05)
GC.res.ABBA.THOC <- results(GC.dds, contrast=c("host_species","ABBA","THOC"), alpha = 0.05)
# Make dataframe for each pairwise comparison ("contrast" results) with significative adj. p values and positive log2FoldChange
GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA <- as.data.frame(GC.res.ABBA.ACSA[is.na(GC.res.ABBA.ACSA$padj)==F&GC.res.ABBA.ACSA$padj<0.05&GC.res.ABBA.ACSA$log2FoldChange>0,])
GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR <- as.data.frame(GC.res.ABBA.FAGR[is.na(GC.res.ABBA.FAGR$padj)==F&GC.res.ABBA.FAGR$padj<0.05&GC.res.ABBA.FAGR$log2FoldChange>0,])
GC.sig.ABBA.COCO <- as.data.frame(GC.res.ABBA.COCO[is.na(GC.res.ABBA.COCO$padj)==F&GC.res.ABBA.COCO$padj<0.05&GC.res.ABBA.COCO$log2FoldChange>0,])
GC.sig.THOC.ACSA <- as.data.frame(GC.res.THOC.ACSA[is.na(GC.res.THOC.ACSA$padj)==F&GC.res.THOC.ACSA$padj<0.05&GC.res.THOC.ACSA$log2FoldChange>0,])
GC.sig.THOC.FAGR <- as.data.frame(GC.res.THOC.FAGR[is.na(GC.res.THOC.FAGR$padj)==F&GC.res.THOC.FAGR$padj<0.05&GC.res.THOC.FAGR$log2FoldChange>0,])
GC.sig.THOC.COCO <- as.data.frame(GC.res.THOC.COCO[is.na(GC.res.THOC.COCO$padj)==F&GC.res.THOC.COCO$padj<0.05&GC.res.THOC.COCO$log2FoldChange>0,])
GC.sig.ABBA.THOC <- as.data.frame(GC.res.ABBA.THOC[is.na(GC.res.ABBA.THOC$padj)==F&GC.res.ABBA.THOC$padj<0.05,])
```

Create dataframe with common DAGs (associated with Gymnosperms) with mean log2 Fold Change and functions
```{r}
# Arrange the 6 results dataframes in decreasing log2FoldChange
GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA))
GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA <- GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA[order(-GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA) <- NULL

GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR))
GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR <- GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR[order(-GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR) <- NULL

GC.sig.ABBA.COCO$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.ABBA.COCO))
GC.sig.ABBA.COCO <- GC.sig.ABBA.COCO[order(-GC.sig.ABBA.COCO$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.ABBA.COCO) <- NULL

GC.sig.THOC.ACSA$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.THOC.ACSA))
GC.sig.THOC.ACSA <- GC.sig.THOC.ACSA[order(-GC.sig.THOC.ACSA$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.THOC.ACSA) <- NULL

GC.sig.THOC.FAGR$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.THOC.FAGR))
GC.sig.THOC.FAGR <- GC.sig.THOC.FAGR[order(-GC.sig.THOC.FAGR$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.THOC.FAGR) <- NULL

GC.sig.THOC.COCO$gene_cluster_id <- as.factor(rownames(GC.sig.THOC.COCO))
GC.sig.THOC.COCO <- GC.sig.THOC.COCO[order(-GC.sig.THOC.COCO$log2FoldChange), ]
row.names(GC.sig.THOC.COCO) <- NULL

# List common DAGs in all 6 df (Gymnosperms vs. Angiosperm all species)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.ABBA.COCO$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.FAGR$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.COCO$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : There is no DAG associated with gymnosperm trees vs angiosperm species (including COCO)

# List common DAGs in 4 df (Gymnosperms vs. Angiosperm tree species)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.FAGR$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : There is no DAG associated with gymnosperm trees vs angiosperm trees (excluding COCO)

# List common DAGs (Gymnosperms vs. ACSA)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.ACSA$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : 1 DAG

# List common DAGs (Gymnosperms vs. FAGR)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.FAGR$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : No DAG

# List common DAGs (Gymnosperms vs. COCO)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.ABBA.COCO$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.COCO$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : 91 DAGs  --> will need to remove from these the GC that are DAGs in TreesABTOAS vs. COCO

# List common DAGs in 3 df (ABBA vs. Angiosperm species)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.ABBA.COCO$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : 2 DAGs associated with ABBA, compared to angiosperm species (including COCO)

# List common DAGs in 2 df (for ABBA, no COCO)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : 2 DAGs associated with ABBA
# These 2 DAGS are the same 2 than in ABBA vs. all angios (so do not make a dataframe for this)

# List common DAGs in 3 df (for THOC, with COCO)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.THOC.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.FAGR$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.COCO$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : 0 DAG associated with THOC, compared to angiosperm species (including COCO)

# List common DAGs in 2 df (for THOC, no COCO)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.THOC.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.FAGR$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : 0 DAG associated with THOC, compared to angiosperm trees



#### ABBA ####


# List common DAGs in 3 df (ABBA vs. Angiosperm species)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.ABBA.COCO$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : 2 DAGs associated with ABBA, compared to angiosperm species (including COCO)

# Calculate the mean log2FoldChange of the common DAGs
GC.df.temp <- rbind(GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA, GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR, GC.sig.ABBA.COCO)
GC.df.temp <- GC.df.temp[GC.df.temp$gene_cluster_id %in% GC.list.common.temp, ]
GC.ABBA.Angio <- aggregate(log2FoldChange ~ gene_cluster_id, data = GC.df.temp, FUN = mean) # Calculate mean log2FoldChange for each common GC
colnames(GC.ABBA.Angio)[2] <- "mean_log2FC"
GC.ABBA.Angio <- GC.ABBA.Angio[order(-GC.ABBA.Angio$mean_log2FC), ] # Order GC in decreasing mean log2FoldChange
rm(GC.list.temp, GC.list.common.temp, GC.df.temp) # remove temp objects

# Add comparisons
# MANUAL ENTER, VERIFY IF SOMETHING CHANGE EN AMONT!!
GC.ABBA.Angio$ABBA <- rep(1,nrow(GC.COCO.Trees))
GC.ABBA.Angio$THOC <- rep(0,nrow(GC.COCO.Trees)) 
GC.ABBA.Angio$ACSA <- rep(-1,nrow(GC.COCO.Trees))
GC.ABBA.Angio$FAGR <- rep(-1,nrow(GC.COCO.Trees))
GC.ABBA.Angio$COCO <- rep(-1,nrow(GC.COCO.Trees))

# Merge DAGs with function and coverage df
GC.ABBA.Angio.fct <- merge(GC.rar.fct.cov, GC.ABBA.Angio, by = "gene_cluster_id")

# Keep rows with unique functional annotation
GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct <- GC.ABBA.Angio.fct %>%
  distinct(gene_cluster_id, COG20_FUNCTION, KOfam, Pfam, CAZyme, Description, .keep_all = TRUE)
GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct <- GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct[,-c(2:3)] # remove gene caller id and genome name because irrelevant
GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct <- GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct[c(1:3,63,4:62,64:68)] # rearrange column order
GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct <- GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct[order(-GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct$mean_log2FC), ] # place rows in order of decreasing mean_log2FC
row.names(GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct) <- NULL
GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct <- droplevels(GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct)
str(GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct)


# List common DAGs in 2 df (for ABBA, no COCO)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : 2 DAGs associated with ABBA
# These 2 DAGS are the same 2 than in ABBA vs. all angios (so do not make a dataframe for this)


# Remove from 'GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct' GC that are also in 'GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct'
which(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct$gene_cluster_id %in% GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct$gene_cluster_id)
GC.levels.temp <- levels(GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct$gene_cluster_id)
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct.only <- subset(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct, !(gene_cluster_id %in% GC.levels.temp))
row.names(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct.only) <- NULL
GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct.only <- droplevels(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct.only)
str(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct.only)
rm(GC.levels.temp)



#### THOC ####


# List common DAGs in 3 df (for THOC, with COCO)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.THOC.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.FAGR$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.COCO$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : 0 DAG associated with THOC, compared to angiosperm species (including COCO)

# List common DAGs in 2 df (for THOC, no COCO)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.THOC.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.FAGR$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : 0 DAG associated with THOC, compared to angiosperm trees






#### Gymnosperms vs. ACSA

# List common DAGs (Gymnosperms vs. ACSA)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.ACSA$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : 1 DAG

# Calculate the mean log2FoldChange of the common DAGs
GC.df.temp <- rbind(GC.sig.ABBA.ACSA,  GC.sig.THOC.ACSA)
GC.df.temp <- GC.df.temp[GC.df.temp$gene_cluster_id %in% GC.list.common.temp, ]
GC.Gymno.ACSA <- aggregate(log2FoldChange ~ gene_cluster_id, data = GC.df.temp, FUN = mean) # Calculate mean log2FoldChange for each common GC
colnames(GC.Gymno.ACSA)[2] <- "mean_log2FC"
GC.Gymno.ACSA <- GC.Gymno.ACSA[order(-GC.Gymno.ACSA$mean_log2FC), ] # Order GC in decreasing mean log2FoldChange
rm(GC.list.temp, GC.list.common.temp, GC.df.temp) # remove temp objects

# Add comparisons
# MANUAL ENTER, VERIFY IF SOMETHING CHANGE EN AMONT!!
GC.Gymno.ACSA$ABBA <- rep(1,nrow(GC.Gymno.ACSA))
GC.Gymno.ACSA$THOC <- rep(1,nrow(GC.Gymno.ACSA))
GC.Gymno.ACSA$ACSA <- rep(-1,nrow(GC.Gymno.ACSA))
GC.Gymno.ACSA$FAGR <- rep(0,nrow(GC.Gymno.ACSA))
GC.Gymno.ACSA$COCO <- rep(0,nrow(GC.Gymno.ACSA))

# Merge DAGs with function and coverage df
GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct <- merge(GC.rar.fct.cov, GC.Gymno.ACSA, by = "gene_cluster_id")

# Keep rows with unique functional annotation
GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct.distinct <- GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct %>%
  distinct(gene_cluster_id, COG20_FUNCTION, KOfam, Pfam, CAZyme, Description, .keep_all = TRUE)
GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct.distinct <- GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct.distinct[,-c(2:3)] # remove gene caller id and genome name because irrelevant
GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct.distinct <- GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct.distinct[c(1:3,63,4:62,64:68)] # rearrange column order
GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct.distinct <- GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct.distinct[order(-GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct.distinct$mean_log2FC), ] # place rows in order of decreasing mean_log2FC
row.names(GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct.distinct) <- NULL
GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct.distinct <- droplevels(GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct.distinct)
str(GC.Gymno.ACSA.fct.distinct)






#### Gymnosperms vs. FAGR

# List common DAGs (Gymnosperms vs. FAGR)
GC.list.temp <- list(GC.sig.ABBA.FAGR$gene_cluster_id, GC.sig.THOC.FAGR$gene_cluster_id)
GC.list.common.temp <- Reduce(intersect, GC.list.temp)
# Result : No DAG






#### Gymnosperms vs. COCO

# List common DAGs (Gymnosperms vs. COCO)

# Result : 91 DAGs

# Calculate the mean log2FoldChange of the common DAGs

GC.df.temp <- GC.df.temp[GC.df.temp$gene_cluster_id %in% GC.list.common.temp, ]

colnames(GC.Gymno.COCO)[2] <- "mean_log2FC"
GC.Gymno.COCO <- GC.Gymno.COCO[order(-GC.Gymno.COCO$mean_log2FC), ] # Order GC in decreasing mean log2FoldChange
rm(GC.list.temp, GC.list.common.temp, GC.df.temp) # remove temp objects

# Add comparisons

GC.Gymno.COCO$ABBA <- rep(1,nrow(GC.Gymno.COCO))
GC.Gymno.COCO$THOC <- rep(1,nrow(GC.Gymno.COCO))
GC.Gymno.COCO$ACSA <- rep(0,nrow(GC.Gymno.COCO))
GC.Gymno.COCO$FAGR <- rep(0,nrow(GC.Gymno.COCO))
GC.Gymno.COCO$COCO <- rep(-1,nrow(GC.Gymno.COCO))

# Merge DAGs with function and coverage df
GC.Gymno.COCO.fct <- merge(GC.rar.fct.cov, GC.Gymno.COCO, by = "gene_cluster_id")

# Keep rows with unique functional annotation
GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct <- GC.Gymno.COCO.fct %>%
  distinct(gene_cluster_id, COG20_FUNCTION, KOfam, Pfam, CAZyme, Description, .keep_all = TRUE)
GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct <- GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct[,-c(2:3)] # remove gene caller id and genome name because irrelevant
GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct <- GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct[c(1:3,63,4:62,64:68)] # rearrange column order
GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct <- GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct[order(-GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct$mean_log2FC), ] # place rows in order of decreasing mean_log2FC
row.names(GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct) <- NULL
GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct <- droplevels(GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct)
str(GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct)

# Remove from 'GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct' GC that are also in 'GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct'
which(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct$gene_cluster_id %in% GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct$gene_cluster_id)
GC.levels.temp <- levels(GC.TreesABTOAS.COCO.fct.distinct$gene_cluster_id)
GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct.only <- subset(GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct, !(gene_cluster_id %in% GC.levels.temp))
row.names(GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct.only) <- NULL
GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct.only <- droplevels(GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct.only)
str(GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct.only)
rm(GC.levels.temp)

# Remove from 'GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct.only' GC that are also in 'GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct'
which(GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct$gene_cluster_id %in% GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct.only$gene_cluster_id)
GC.levels.temp <- levels(GC.ABBA.Angio.fct.distinct$gene_cluster_id)
GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct.only <- subset(GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct.only, !(gene_cluster_id %in% GC.levels.temp))
row.names(GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct.only) <- NULL
GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct.only <- droplevels(GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct.only)
str(GC.Gymno.COCO.fct.distinct.only)
rm(GC.levels.temp)
```
